summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 7 warning, 4 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 PLAYBOOK.md:199 — Slug description says "non-alphanumeric characters stripped" but `run.mjs:159` uses `/[^a-z0-9\-\.]/g` and `worktree.test.mjs:37` explicitly tests that dots are preserved (`slugToBranch("v1.0") === "v1.0"`). Update to: "only alphanumeric, hyphens, and dots retained (all other characters stripped), capped at 72 characters."

🟡 PLAYBOOK.md:200 — "uses `-B` so re-running resets the branch to HEAD" is false; `run.mjs:169–172` shows `existsSync(worktreePath)` causes an early return before `git worktree add -B` is ever invoked on a re-run; fix: "branch `feature/<slug>` is created on first run with `git worktree add -B`; re-runs reuse the existing worktree and preserve all commits"

🟡 `PLAYBOOK.md:201` — "re-running resets the branch to HEAD" is factually wrong. `createWorktreeIfNeeded` (`bin/lib/run.mjs:169`) returns early when the directory already exists — `-B` is never invoked on retry. This directly contradicts the crash-recovery behavior (task-5). Fix: "uses `-B` on initial creation only; re-runs reuse the existing worktree and preserve all commits."
🟡 `.team/features/git-worktree-isolation/tasks/task-12/handshake.json` — No `test-output.txt` artifact provided. The claimed "558 tests pass" cannot be independently verified from artifacts alone.

🟡 `PLAYBOOK.md:200` — "uses `-B` so re-running resets the branch to HEAD" is false for crash-recovery reuse. `createWorktreeIfNeeded` in `run.mjs:167–168` short-circuits with `return worktreePath` when the directory exists — `git worktree add -B` is never called, and the branch is not reset. Fix to clarify `-B` applies only on initial creation.

🟡 `test/worktree.test.mjs` — Zero tests assert that PLAYBOOK.md contains the `## Git Worktrees` section or its key commands. If the section is accidentally deleted or renamed, no test catches it. Add a minimal doc-contract test reading the file and asserting presence of the section heading and key commands (`git worktree remove --force`, `git worktree prune`).

🟡 `PLAYBOOK.md:200` — "uses `-B` so re-running resets the branch to HEAD" is factually wrong. The code (`run.mjs:169-172`) exits early and reuses the worktree if the path exists — the `-B` flag is never reached in the crash-recovery re-run case. The phrase should describe the *initial creation* scenario (where the branch already exists from a prior run that was manually pruned), not re-running in general.
